auditor: external evaluator (read-only; no production code changed)
method: lead-auditor first-hand verification (pytest, §8 validate_task x24, SQLite integrity, byte-reproducibility, Wilson recompute, forensic-cohort consistency) + 13-agent adversarial re-audit workflow (4 closure/forensic/new-defect agents + 9 dimension rescores); every agent finding reconciled and false positives discarded by the lead auditor.
maturity: trustworthy research-grade evaluation benchmark + local CLI harness; NOT product-ready; NOT safe for untrusted agents
verdict_one_line: Audit-1's three P0 integrity defects are genuinely closed under independent adversarial re-derivation — the published leaderboard is now a faithful, byte-reproducible projection of 600 real persisted runs. What remains is structural and disclosed: no untrusted isolation, no product surface, a legacy forensic gap, and minor latent nits.

headline.p1_status: P1-1 closed (forward) / P1-2 closed / P1-4 closed / P1-3 open / P1-5 open
headline.biggest_strength: Evaluation validity recovered: 600 real runs, forensic cohort 100% score/test-consistent, persisted patches trace to real task semantics; the leaderboard regenerates byte-identically from the committed DB.
headline.biggest_weakness: Still a CLI/library with no product surface (no dashboard/API/run-viewer/CONTRIBUTING) and no untrusted-agent isolation (DockerSandbox unimplemented).
headline.most_dangerous_unresolved_risk: Anti-gaming guarantees hold only under the trusted-local-agent assumption: hidden tests are readable on the shared FS during agent.act and no DockerSandbox exists. Fine for the current local eval; blocking for any untrusted/public use.
headline.next_mandatory_step: None blocking for a research/portfolio benchmark. To advance maturity: (a) re-evaluate the 3 v1.0.1 tasks so their published cells match their current hidden suites, and (b) implement DockerSandbox before admitting untrusted agents.

verified_facts.validate_task: 24/24 tasks pass §8 (reference 1.0 deterministic 3x; empty diff fails correctly)
verified_facts.db: 600 real runs = 5 models x 24 tasks x 5; 0 recon hashes, 0 voided, 0 duplicates, 0 synthetic/oracle/noop rows in DB
verified_facts.leaderboard_lcb: all recompute exactly: qwen7b 0.477, deepseek 0.240, qwen3b 0.196, llama 0.152, gemma2 0.023; oracle 0.969 / noop 0.000 are render-only synthetic baselines
verified_facts.byte_reproducible: report_combined.py regenerated 3x => identical leaderboard.html SHA 87ca6c77... matching the committed file; runs.sqlite SHA 519bee8a...
verified_facts.forensic_cohort: last 100 runs persist patch_text + test_results (1218 rows / 99 runs); 0 functional_pass-with-hidden-fail, 0 G1-allpass-but-not-pass, 0 t_hidden/per-test mismatch; inspected patches trace to real task semantics
verified_facts.hidden_suite_strengthening: async-batched 5->9-11, top-k-frequent 12->16, refactor-order-validation 1->21 hidden tests (all v1.0.1, still §8-valid)

partially_closed: P2-5 (3 task hidden suites strengthened, but their committed leaderboard cells are still v1.0.0 evidence — disclosed via version badge, not re-evaluated)
still_open: P1-3 (no DockerSandbox / untrusted isolation; hidden tests readable during act)
P1-1 legacy half (first 500 runs lack patch/test detail, irrecoverable)

closures_verified_genuine.evidence: report_combined.py is DB-first; KNOWN_OLD/recon absent; DB has 600 real rows, 0 synthetic-named; pass counts match. Mutation-tested via P1-4.
runs.sqlite + leaderboard.html git-tracked; regenerated 3x byte-identical (SHA 87ca6c77); only date is DB-derived run-window, not wall-clock.
all 5 models have >=5 tasks AND >=25 real runs in every domain (api/async/perf/security 25 runs, backend 85); 0 cells gap-filled.
GradeReport threaded pipeline->store->eval_persist; last 100 runs have 100 patches + 1218 test rows; first 500 NULL (legacy, irrecoverable).
README v0.2.0, llama3.2:latest@a80c4f17acd5, python3; leaderboard matches DB; 500->600 transition dated in FAILURE_INSPECTION.
test_report_combined.py proven non-smoke by mutation: injecting a recon row makes it FAIL (assert 2==1).

BOTH 01-run-scoring.md and EVALUATION_FRAMEWORK.md now use 2x/8x added-only with §1.6 example S=0.6659; grep finds no residual 4x/10x/0.6711. (One re-audit agent falsely claimed EVALUATION_FRAMEWORK.md was unfixed; refuted by grep at lines 163-235.)
diffing.py normalizes / and \\ and uses casefold(); tests cover Conftest.py/CONFTEST.PY/.PTH variants.
snapshot_tree skips any path with a symlink component; tested for file and dir symlinks.
report tests now assert numeric Wilson values/rank ranges + counts; test_cross_domain_integration added.
README/docs/report disclose backend 17/24 and api/perf 3 primary tasks, with tag weights and a coverage caveat.
baseline_adjusted_t_hidden defined + tested but deliberately NOT wired into score_run (formula v0.1 unchanged).
report.py counts a domain task only when its aggregate has >=1 valid run.

P2.title: Production task-version resolution path is untested
P2.detail: manifest.json items carry no 'version' key (0/24), so _current_task_version() falls back to reading task.json from disk in production — but both tests pass 'version' inline, exercising only the dict branch. The disk-read path that actually runs in production is uncovered.

P3.title: build_report silently EXCLUDES any real agent not in the hardcoded MODELS list
P3.detail: Direction is under-reporting (safe), and currently moot (DB agents == MODELS). Latent footgun if a new model is evaluated but not added to MODELS.

P3.title: save_run silently persists detail-less rows when report is None
P3.detail: No guard/assert; unreachable in the production path (run_once/eval_persist always attach grade_report) but a defensive assertion would harden P1-1.

P3.title: Synthetic oracle/noop injected at v1.0.1 while real evidence is v1.0.0, pooling two versions for the 3 hardened tasks
P3.detail: Narrows the 'refuse mixed versions' guarantee for those 3 cells; cosmetic since oracle/noop are synthetic 1.0/0.0 anchors.

P3.title: Stale-version evidence under-disclosed: v1.0.0 data under a 'current v1.0.1' badge with the discrepancy only in a hover tooltip
P3.detail: The 3 hardened tasks' published cells reflect v1.0.0 grading; disclosed but easy to miss.

P3.title: Synthetic oracle/noop rank identically to measured agents with only an inline name-string label
P3.detail: A reader skimming the table could mistake them for measured agents; the only differentiator is the '(synthetic baseline)' suffix.

P3.title: store load_runs/summary INNER-JOIN runs to diffs with no guard for a diff-less run
P3.detail: A run row without a diff row would be silently dropped from loads/aggregates. Currently moot (every run has a diff).

P3.title: Empty-diff runs recorded as status='valid' (G=0) rather than the doc-specified AGENT_ERROR
P3.detail: Spec/impl divergence; scoring is correct either way (G=0 => S=0), but the status taxonomy differs from the framework text.
P3.evidence: pipeline.py status assignment vs framework run-status taxonomy

P3.title: Observability 'runs with patch artifacts' counts 5 empty-string patches as present
P3.detail: 100/600 'with patches' includes 5 no-edit runs whose patch_text is '' (present but empty); a minor accuracy nuance in the coverage stat.

P3.title: Two parallel canonical framework docs (modular 01-run-scoring.md + consolidated EVALUATION_FRAMEWORK.md) without a source-of-truth marker
P3.detail: Both are currently consistent (parsimony reconciled in both), but maintaining two copies is a drift hazard.

domain_table_real_db.note: pooled weighted pass rate per (model,domain) from real DB; all cells now displayable (>=5 tasks AND >=25 runs)

reproducibility_checklist.infra_failures_recorded_separately: PARTIAL (no infra/voided rows in this run; mechanism present and tested)
reproducibility_checklist.per_run_transcript_or_model_digest_store: FAIL (not persisted; LLM generations not bit-reproducible)

should_do: Re-evaluate the 3 v1.0.1 tasks (async-batched, top-k-frequent, refactor-order-validation) so their published per-task cells match their current hidden suites; or make the stale-version disclosure more prominent than a tooltip.
Add a defensive assert in save_run when a real run is persisted without a GradeReport (hardens P1-1 against future regressions).
Cover the production task-version resolution path (disk read) with a test; add a manifest 'version' key or test the fallback explicitly.
optional: Persist per-run model digest + transcript so generations are reproducible, not just aggregates.
Render oracle/noop visually distinct from measured agents (not just a name suffix).
Pick one canonical framework doc (modular vs consolidated) and mark the other as generated/derived to remove the drift hazard.
Floor T_hidden against the snapshot baseline (wire baseline_adjusted_t_hidden) under a formula-version bump + leaderboard recompute.
do_not_do_yet: Do not admit untrusted agents until DockerSandbox (or equivalent FS/network isolation) is implemented.
Do not build the Postgres/Next.js dashboard before deciding whether the static report + DB already meet the observability need.
